title: Exact Quantum Many-Body Scars by a generalized Matrix-Product Ansatz
authors: Sascha Gehrmann, Fabian H. L. Essler
abstract: We construct exact eigenstates of quantum many-body systems with Hamiltonians that are not frustration-free in matrix product form, based on a local error cancellation ansatz motivated by the Derrida-Evans-Hakim-Pasquier method for finding the stationary state of the asymmetric simple exclusion process. We demonstrate the approach with explicit examples in both one and two spatial dimensions.
summary: This paper presents a new mathematical framework to find exact Matrix Product State eigenstates for Hamiltonians that are not frustration-free. By using a local error cancellation technique, the authors demonstrate how to construct quantum many-body scars in 1D and 2D spin systems. This expands the toolkit for studying non-thermalizing quantum dynamics.
main_problem: Developing a general method to construct exact Matrix Product State (MPS) eigenstates for quantum many-body systems where the Hamiltonian is not frustration-free.
main_result: The authors successfully constructed exact zero-energy MPS eigenstates (quantum many-body scars) in both 1D and 2D by using a local error cancellation ansatz.
method: A generalized Matrix Product Ansatz (MPA) based on a local error cancellation condition, inspired by the DEHP method for the ASEP, which reduces the problem to finding representations of a quadratic algebra.
model_or_system: The study covers various models including 1D spin-S chains with Dzyalimoshinskii-Moriya interactions, 1D spin-1 chains, and 2D square lattice models (spin-2 and spin-1 XYZ models).
key_observables: Energy eigenvalues (specifically zero-energy states), two-point correlation functions, and correlation length.
important_parameters: Spin magnitude S, Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction components (Dx, Dy, Dz), magnetic field strengths (hz, hy), and a complex parameter 'a' used in the expansion.
main_assumptions: The construction relies on the existence of a local error cancellation mechanism and translational invariance; the method is demonstrated for specific fine-tuned boundary conditions in open chains.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a generalized DEHP-based ansatz, applies it to 1D spin chains (Model I and II), extends the construction to 2D square lattice models, and discusses the implications for quantum many-body scars and computational utility.
why_interesting: The discovery of exact, low-entanglement eigenstates (scars) in non-frustration-free systems provides a powerful tool for studying non-thermal dynamics and offers states that are potentially observable on near-term quantum computers.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
